-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","title":"[T1-MSG.1.1] WhatsApp: types, client, error modules","description":"Create connectors/whatsapp/ crate scaffold with:
- src/types.rs: Message types (TextMessage, MediaMessage, TemplateMessage,
  InteractiveMessage, LocationMessage, ContactMessage), webhook payload types
  (WebhookEntry, WebhookChange, WebhookValue, IncomingMessage, MessageStatus),
  API response types (SendMessageResponse, ProfileResponse)
- src/client.rs: WhatsAppClient with ConnectorRuntime, HttpRetryConfig,
  RetryLoop::execute for all API calls, auth header injection, base URL
  configuration, rate limit handling (429 → Retryable with retry-after)
- src/error.rs: WhatsAppError enum (Http, Json, Api, RateLimited, Unauthorized,
  InvalidPhoneNumber, TemplateRejected) with ConnectorErrorMapping impl
- Cargo.toml with fcp-core, fcp-sdk, fcp-async-core, reqwest, serde, serde_json,
  thiserror, tracing, wiremock (dev), chrono deps

## Evidence required
- cargo check passes
- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings clean
- Error Display/Debug formats tested
- ConnectorErrorMapping roundtrip tested","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:18.241245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:40:16.471368Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:02.861395Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:26.175977Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:40.604707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:22:26.339129Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:40:16.470612Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","title":"[T1-MSG.1.2] WhatsApp: connector impl + manifest + operations","description":"Implement WhatsApp connector struct, manifest, operations dispatch, introspection with typed OperationInfo for all 8 ops (send_text, send_media, send_template, send_interactive, send_location, mark_read, get_profile, update_profile). Config parsing with access_token/credential_id, phone_number_id, webhook_verify_token. Binary entrypoint with JSON-RPC stdio loop. Manifest with graph.facebook.com network constraint and provisioning recipe. Evidence: all operations dispatch correctly, introspection returns correct count, manifest passes validation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:45.348919Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:54.683581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:17.314429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:45.348919Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:33:54.682508Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.4","title":"[T1-MSG.1.3] WhatsApp: webhook handler + signature verification","description":"Webhook handling for incoming WhatsApp messages: challenge-response verification (hub.mode/hub.verify_token/hub.challenge), HMAC-SHA256 signature verification using app secret, payload parsing into typed IncomingMessage events, event routing for all message types (text, image, video, audio, document, location, contacts, interactive/button reply, status update), replay detection via message ID dedup, dead letter queue. Uses fcp-webhook WebhookHandler with WhatsAppSignatureVerifier. Security: app secret never in logs, verify_token cryptographically random, all payloads signature-verified, phone numbers partially redacted in logs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:58.569952Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:31.121603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:31.121018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.4","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:53:58.569952Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","title":"[T1-MSG.1.4] WhatsApp: tests (50+ unit, 10+ integration)","description":"Comprehensive WhatsApp test suite. Unit (50+, wiremock): send text/media/template/interactive, mark_read, profile ops, retry behavior (429/500), error mapping all variants, webhook parsing all types, webhook signature valid/invalid/missing, config parsing edge cases. Integration (10+): full send-receive cycle, webhook verify challenge, ConnectorSuite introspection, ComplianceSuite default-deny, rate limit exhaustion/recovery, shutdown. All deterministic, no real API calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:12.795445Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T00:40:31.170656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:32:44.941429Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-17T21:54:12.795445Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:34:08.599081Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:34:23.100876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:34:37.558485Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:40:31.169182Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Pin down the exact first-slice service inventory across messages, chats, users, docs or drive where in scope, webhook or websocket event surfaces, the auth boundary around tenants and apps, OperationInfo risk semantics, and explicit non-goals for the first implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:22.684168Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:22.684168Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:22.684168Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:22.684168Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:22.684168Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","title":"Feishu / Lark: typed client foundation, runtime, manifest, and service modules","description":"Build the Feishu or Lark connector implementation: typed config and auth, messaging and tenant client modules, event handling where in scope, ConnectorRuntime lifecycle, truthful manifest declarations, capability scope, network constraints, and typed introspection for the chosen Feishu surfaces.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:38.484691Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.3","title":"Feishu / Lark: readiness, compliance, verification, and operator guidance","description":"Finish Feishu or Lark with readiness and doctor outputs, tenant and app remediation guidance, deterministic tests across messaging and event workflows, and explicit V3 compliance evidence for an APAC workplace-platform connector.","acceptance_criteria":"- closeout requires comprehensive deterministic unit coverage for auth, timeout, retry, error-mapping, pagination or state transitions, and the highest-risk provider-specific mutations
- closeout requires archetype-appropriate integration coverage built on shared `fcp-testkit` helpers rather than connector-local one-offs
- closeout requires at least one replayable E2E or host-backed verification script that runs through `rch`, captures detailed structured logs, and preserves manifest, readiness, doctor, and conformance artifacts
- operator guidance must name prerequisites, dedicated environments, redaction rules, common remediation paths, and rerun commands for the verification bundle
- evidence attached to the bead must be sufficient for a future agent to rerun verification without reopening external planning notes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:54.345584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:28:29.701094Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:54.345584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:54.345584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.8.7.3","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-18T01:27:54.345584Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","title":"[CROSS-V3] Connector Test Infrastructure: unit/integration/E2E harnesses, detailed logging, compliance suite","description":"# Cross-Cutting Test Infrastructure

## Goal
Establish shared V3-native test patterns and a reusable compliance harness so both new connectors and existing connectors are verified against the current Flywheel Connector Protocol V3 contract instead of legacy ad hoc expectations.

## Why this bead exists early
The workspace spans messaging, cloud, database, developer platform, productivity, commerce, communication, and local-host/process archetypes. Without shared scaffolding, each connector team or retrofit pass would re-invent retry tests, webhook fixtures, manifest validation, readiness checks, and conformance evidence.

## Deliverables

### 1. Connector scaffold template update
Update `fwc` scaffold generation so new connector crates start V3-native by default:
- `ConnectorRuntime`, `RetryLoop`, and `ConnectorErrorMapping` already wired into the generated client and error paths
- wiremock-based unit test scaffolding for success, retry, timeout, cancellation, auth failure, and config validation
- typed `OperationInfo` stubs with `SafetyTier`, `RiskLevel`, and `IdempotencyClass`
- manifest skeleton with capabilities, network constraints, provisioning notes, and readiness hooks

### 2. Shared test helpers in `fcp-testkit`
Provide reusable helpers covering the archetypes in this campaign and in the existing connector inventory:
- HTTP request-response fixtures
- webhook/signature verification fixtures
- bridge/daemon fixtures for Signal-, BlueBubbles-, and similar local-bridge connectors
- database and storage fixture helpers
- assertions for health/readiness/doctor outputs and retry telemetry

### 3. Reusable connector compliance script
Create a reusable compliance entrypoint that:
- boots a connector binary
- exercises handshake/configure/introspect/invoke/shutdown
- validates manifest/readiness surfaces
- emits structured logs/evidence for `fcp-conformance` and E2E review

This must be usable for retrofitting existing connectors, not just for greenfield scaffolds.

### 4. Documentation and operator guidance
Write the campaign-level test playbook with:
- minimum unit/integration/E2E coverage expectations by archetype
- how to run verification through `rch`
- when a bridge dependency requires dedicated integration environments
- how readiness/doctor/conformance evidence should be attached to implementation or remediation beads

## Acceptance criteria
- A newly scaffolded V3 connector starts with meaningful tests instead of placeholder stubs
- Shared helpers exist for HTTP, webhook, bridge, database, and readiness validation paths
- Multiple representative existing connectors from different archetypes can consume the compliance harness as proof the retrofit path is real
- The playbook points future contributors at the V3 spec, README, `fcp-conformance`, `fcp-testkit`, and `fcp-e2e` as the canonical evidence surfaces","acceptance_criteria":"- scaffolded connectors include archetype-appropriate unit tests, integration smoke tests, replayable E2E or compliance entrypoints, and structured log-capture hooks by default
- `fcp-testkit` provides reusable fixtures for HTTP, webhook, bridge or daemon, database or storage, readiness or doctor, and artifact or log assertions
- the shared compliance harness emits machine-readable logs, manifest or readiness snapshots, and replayable evidence bundles consumable by `fcp-conformance` and `fcp-e2e`
- the campaign playbook defines per-archetype minimum unit, integration, and E2E thresholds plus redaction and logging expectations
- at least one representative existing connector in each major archetype proves retrofit compatibility with the shared harness","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:21:47.277167Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T06:39:51.527277Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:21:47.277167Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","title":"[CROSS-V3] Connector Test Infrastructure: unit/integration/E2E harnesses, detailed logging, compliance suite","description":"# Cross-Cutting Test Infrastructure

## Goal
Establish shared V3-native test patterns and a reusable compliance harness so both new connectors and existing connectors are verified against the current Flywheel Connector Protocol V3 contract instead of legacy ad hoc expectations.

## Why this bead exists early
The workspace spans messaging, cloud, database, developer platform, productivity, commerce, communication, and local-host/process archetypes. Without shared scaffolding, each connector team or retrofit pass would re-invent retry tests, webhook fixtures, manifest validation, readiness checks, and conformance evidence.

## Deliverables

### 1. Connector scaffold template update
Update `fwc` scaffold generation so new connector crates start V3-native by default:
- `ConnectorRuntime`, `RetryLoop`, and `ConnectorErrorMapping` already wired into the generated client and error paths
- wiremock-based unit test scaffolding for success, retry, timeout, cancellation, auth failure, and config validation
- typed `OperationInfo` stubs with `SafetyTier`, `RiskLevel`, and `IdempotencyClass`
- manifest skeleton with capabilities, network constraints, provisioning notes, and readiness hooks

### 2. Shared test helpers in `fcp-testkit`
Provide reusable helpers covering the archetypes in this campaign and in the existing connector inventory:
- HTTP request-response fixtures
- webhook/signature verification fixtures
- bridge/daemon fixtures for Signal-, BlueBubbles-, and similar local-bridge connectors
- database and storage fixture helpers
- assertions for health/readiness/doctor outputs and retry telemetry

### 3. Reusable connector compliance script
Create a reusable compliance entrypoint that:
- boots a connector binary
- exercises handshake/configure/introspect/invoke/shutdown
- validates manifest/readiness surfaces
- emits structured logs/evidence for `fcp-conformance` and E2E review

This must be usable for retrofitting existing connectors, not just for greenfield scaffolds.

### 4. Documentation and operator guidance
Write the campaign-level test playbook with:
- minimum unit/integration/E2E coverage expectations by archetype
- how to run verification through `rch`
- when a bridge dependency requires dedicated integration environments
- how readiness/doctor/conformance evidence should be attached to implementation or remediation beads

## Acceptance criteria
- A newly scaffolded V3 connector starts with meaningful tests instead of placeholder stubs
- Shared helpers exist for HTTP, webhook, bridge, database, and readiness validation paths
- Multiple representative existing connectors from different archetypes can consume the compliance harness as proof the retrofit path is real
- The playbook points future contributors at the V3 spec, README, `fcp-conformance`, `fcp-testkit`, and `fcp-e2e` as the canonical evidence surfaces","acceptance_criteria":"- scaffolded connectors include archetype-appropriate unit tests, integration smoke tests, replayable E2E or compliance entrypoints, and structured log-capture hooks by default
- `fcp-testkit` provides reusable fixtures for HTTP, webhook, bridge or daemon, database or storage, readiness or doctor, and artifact or log assertions
- the shared compliance harness emits machine-readable logs, manifest or readiness snapshots, and replayable evidence bundles consumable by `fcp-conformance` and `fcp-e2e`
- the campaign playbook defines per-archetype minimum unit, integration, and E2E thresholds plus redaction and logging expectations
- at least one representative existing connector in each major archetype proves retrofit compatibility with the shared harness","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:21:47.277167Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:32.722248Z","closed_at":"2026-03-18T07:24:32.721696Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-j05nu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-18T00:21:47.277167Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
